devanagari: श्रीपरीक्षिदुवाच । निवृत्तिमार्गः कथित आदौ भगवता यथा । क्रमयोगोपलब्धेन ब्रह्मणा यदसंसृतिः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīparīkṣiduvācanivṛttimārgaḥ kathita ādau bhagavatā yathākramayogopalabdhena brahmaṇā yadasaṃsṛtiḥ
translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) said:
The path of cessation from karmas (nivṛtti) has been already described in detail, by your respectable self, in the beginning.[1] By that path, Brahmā (i.e. the region of Brahmā) is gradually attained through different stages[2] (such as passing through the abode of fire-god etc.). The Liberation is attained along with god Brahmā.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Vide Supra 2.2.24 in Vaiśvānaraṃ yāti and 3.32.5 in Ye svadharmān na druhyanti.

Footnote [2]:
Karma-yoga: (i) Through the path of action (Karma), knowledge (Jñāna) and devotion (Bhakti)—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Remembrance of the Lord, visualization of the various members of the Lord’s Person and visualization of the full divine form—Padaratnāvalī
(iii) Birth in a good family, residence at a holy place, association with saintly persons (S.D.)
(iv) dhyāna (contemplation), dhāraṇā (concentration) and samādhi (realization of identity with Brahman.) Bālaprabodhini

devanagari: प्रवृत्तिलक्षणश्चैव त्रैगुण्यविषयो मुने । योऽसावलीनप्रकृतेः गुणसर्गः पुनः पुनः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: pravṛttilakṣaṇaścaiva traiguṇyaviṣayo muneyo'sāvalīnaprakṛteḥ guṇasargaḥ punaḥ punaḥ
translation: And (you have also described in detail)—the path characterised by worldly activities (pravṛtti), the object of which is to attain happiness in heaven and other pleasures which are themselves the product of guṇas[3] and which, in the case of jīvas, not disassociated with Prakṛti[4], results in constant re-embodiment of the jīvas for repeated enjoyments of such pleasures.[5]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
traiguṇyaṃ svargādisukhaṃ tadeva viṣayaḥ prāpyaṃ yasya—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā.

Footnote [4]:
alīna-prakṛteḥ—avinaṣṭa prakṛti sambandhasya /—Bhāgavata Candrikā.

Footnote [5]:
guṇa-sargaḥ—bhogārthaṃ dehārambha-rūpaḥ (yo mārgaḥ)—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: अधर्मलक्षणा नाना नरकाश्चानुवर्णिताः । मन्वन्तरश्च व्याख्यात आद्यः स्वायम्भुवो यतः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: adharmalakṣaṇā nānā narakāścānuvarṇitāḥmanvantaraśca vyākhyāta ādyaḥ svāyambhuvo yataḥ
translation: The various infernal regions which are characterised by unrighteousness (as their cause) have been described in detail (in the fifth Skandha). And the first period of Manu, (presided over and hence) named after Svāyambhuva Manu, has also been described (in the fourth Skandha).

devanagari: प्रियव्रतोत्तानपदोः वंशस्तत् चरितानि च । द्वीपवर्षसमुद्राद्रि नद्युद्यान वनस्पतीन् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: priyavratottānapadoḥ vaṃśastat caritāni cadvīpavarṣasamudrādri nadyudyāna vanaspatīn
translation: The dynasty of Priyavrata and Uttānapāda and the life-stories of (both of) those kings (have been narrated by you). You have also described the (division of the earth in important) island-continents (dvīpas), their subdivisions (varṣas), oceans, mountains, rivers, gardens, the disposition of this spherical earth with reference to its divisions, their special characteristics and dimensions and also those of heavenly bodies and subterranean regions, as they were created by the Almighty Lord.

devanagari: धरामण्डलसंस्थानं भागलक्षणमानतः । ज्योतिषां विवराणां च यथेदं असृजद्विभुः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: dharāmaṇḍalasaṃsthānaṃ bhāgalakṣaṇamānataḥjyotiṣāṃ vivarāṇāṃ ca yathedaṃ asṛjadvibhuḥ
translation: The dynasty of Priyavrata and Uttānapāda and the life-stories of (both of) those kings (have been narrated by you). You have also described the (division of the earth in important) island-continents (dvīpas), their subdivisions (varṣas), oceans, mountains, rivers, gardens, the disposition of this spherical earth with reference to its divisions, their special characteristics and dimensions and also those of heavenly bodies and subterranean regions, as they were created by the Almighty Lord.

devanagari: अधुनेह महाभाग यथैव नरकान्नरः । नानोग्रयातनान्नेयात् तन्मे व्याख्यातुमर्हसि ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: adhuneha mahābhāga yathaiva narakānnaraḥnānograyātanānneyāt tanme vyākhyātumarhasi
translation: Oh illustrious holy sage! Now be pleased to explain in detail, the way which may save a man from falling into the infernal regions of various unbearable tortures.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । न चेदिहैवापचितिं यथांहसः । कृतस्य कुर्यान् मनौक्तपाणिभिः । ध्रुवं स वै प्रेत्य नरकानुपैति । ये कीर्तिता मे भवतः तिग्मयातना...
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācana cedihaivāpacitiṃ yathāṃhasaḥkṛtasya kuryān manauktapāṇibhiḥdhruvaṃ sa vai pretya narakānupaitiye kīrtitā me bhavataḥ tigmayātanāḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
If a man does not perform expiation (as prescribed in the dharma śāstra), in this very life, for the sin committed by him with his thought, word and deed, it is said that he definitely goes to the hellish regions after death. And those regions of severe tormentations have been described to your honoured self by me.

devanagari: तस्मात्पुरैवाश्विह पापनिष्कृतौ । यतेत मृत्योरविपद्यतात्मना । दोषस्य दृष्ट्वा गुरुलाघवं यथा । भिषक्चिकित्सेत रुजां निदानवित् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmātpuraivāśviha pāpaniṣkṛtauyateta mṛtyoravipadyatātmanādoṣasya dṛṣṭvā gurulāghavaṃ yathābhiṣakcikitseta rujāṃ nidānavit
translation: Hence, before one is overtaken by death and not incapacitated in body (or while one is not entangled in difficulties) one should immediately (after the commission of the sin), try to atone for the sin, with a controlled mind, according to the gravity or simplicity of the nature of the fault, just as a physician expert in diagnosis of the disease, promptly adopts remedies or dosage of the medicine (according to the nature of the disease).

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । दृष्टश्रुताभ्यां यत्पापं जानन् अपि आत्मनोऽहितम् । करोति भूयो विवशः प्रायश्चित्तमथो कथम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovācadṛṣṭaśrutābhyāṃ yatpāpaṃ jānan api ātmano'hitamkaroti bhūyo vivaśaḥ prāyaścittamatho katham
translation:
The King said:
In spite of the full knowledge that (commission of) a sin is against his interest as evidenced by what he has seen (viz. punishment meted out by the king for an offence) and has heard (for example internment in hellish region as described in Purāṇas), he loses the control over himself, commits it repeatedly after the act of expiation. Hence (under these circumstances), how can there be any (real) expiation at all, if the tendency to commit sins, persists.

devanagari: क्वचित् निवर्तते अभद्रात् क्वचित् चरति तत्पुनः । प्रायश्चित्तमथोऽपार्थं मन्ये कुञ्जरशौचवत् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: kvacit nivartate abhadrāt kvacit carati tatpunaḥprāyaścittamatho'pārthaṃ manye kuñjaraśaucavat
translation: Sometimes he refrains from committing a sin; on other occasions, he does it again. Hence, I think that the act of expiation is meaningless like the washing of an elephant (which besmears its body with dust again after washing).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । कर्मणा कर्मनिर्हारो न ह्यात्यन्तिक इष्यते । अविद्वदधिकारित्वात् प्रायश्चित्तं विमर्शनम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācakarmaṇā karmanirhāro na hyātyantika iṣyateavidvadadhikāritvāt prāyaścittaṃ vimarśanam
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
It is not laid down that there is the total and final annihilation of sin by the action of expiation (such as bodily mortification), for only the ignorant (i.e those who wrongly identify body with the Soul) are eligible for it. (But as the ignorance about the Soul and non-Soul—avidyā—is not dispelled,the particular sin may be atoned for, and the man again relapses into sinful ways). Knowledge (i.e. self-realization) alone is the real expiation (as it removes the ignorance—avidyā which is the real root-cause of the sin).

devanagari: नाश्नतः पथ्यमेवान्नं व्याधयोऽभिभवन्ति हि । एवं नियमकृद् राजन् शनैः क्षेमाय कल्पते ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: nāśnataḥ pathyamevānnaṃ vyādhayo'bhibhavanti hievaṃ niyamakṛd rājan śanaiḥ kṣemāya kalpate
translation: Just as diseases do not attack a person who eats only wholesome food, (on the contrary they recede gradually if they had already affected that person), a person who observes regular spiritual discipline (e.g. penance) becomes, step by step, eligible for the summum bonum (which annihilates sin and leads to realization of the self), Oh King.

devanagari: तपसा ब्रह्मचर्येण शमेन च दमेन च । त्यागेन सत्यशौचाभ्यां यमेन नियमेन वा ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tapasā brahmacaryeṇa śamena ca damena catyāgena satyaśaucābhyāṃ yamena niyamena vā
translation: Through concentration of the mind and senseorgans, practice of (eightfold) celibacy, the control of the mind and external organs, charitable gifts, truthfulness, purity (both bodily and mental), the vows of non-violence etc. and observances like japa (muttering the sacred syllable OM), the wise (who bear with equanimity the pairs of opposites like pleasure and pain) who understand (real principles of) righteousness and are full of faith, rid themselves of the most grievous sin committed by thought, word or action, just as the fire destroys even a big cluster of bamboos.

devanagari: देहवाग्बुद्धिजं धीरा धर्मज्ञाः श्रद्धयान्विताः । क्षिपन्त्यघं महदपि वेणुगुल्ममिवानलः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: dehavāgbuddhijaṃ dhīrā dharmajñāḥ śraddhayānvitāḥkṣipantyaghaṃ mahadapi veṇugulmamivānalaḥ
translation: Through concentration of the mind and senseorgans, practice of (eightfold) celibacy, the control of the mind and external organs, charitable gifts, truthfulness, purity (both bodily and mental), the vows of non-violence etc. and observances like japa (muttering the sacred syllable OM), the wise (who bear with equanimity the pairs of opposites like pleasure and pain) who understand (real principles of) righteousness and are full of faith, rid themselves of the most grievous sin committed by thought, word or action, just as the fire destroys even a big cluster of bamboos.

devanagari: केचित्केवलया भक्त्या वासुदेवपरायणाः । अघं धुन्वन्ति कार्त्स्न्येन नीहारमिव भास्करः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: kecitkevalayā bhaktyā vāsudevaparāyaṇāḥaghaṃ dhunvanti kārtsnyena nīhāramiva bhāskaraḥ
translation: Just as the Sun completely dispels the mist, a few persons absolutely devoted to Lord Vāsudeva, annihilate their sins thoroughly (from its very root of avidyā) merely through their unswerving devotion to the Lord (without performing penance etc. But the number of such devotees is scarce).

devanagari: न तथा ह्यघवान् राजन् पूयेत तप आदिभिः । यथा कृष्णार्पितप्राणः तत्पूरुषनिषेवया ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: na tathā hyaghavān rājan pūyeta tapa ādibhiḥyathā kṛṣṇārpitaprāṇaḥ tatpūruṣaniṣevayā
translation: Oh King! A sinner is not purified to that extent through performance of penance and expiatory acts as a person who has dedicated his life to Lord Kṛṣṇa (and regards Kṛṣṇa as his last resort, without worrying over the punishment in hell) does so by rendering service to his devotees.

devanagari: सध्रीचीनो ह्ययं लोके पन्थाः क्षेमोऽकुतोभयः । सुशीलाः साधवो यत्र नारायणपरायणाः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: sadhrīcīno hyayaṃ loke panthāḥ kṣemo'kutobhayaḥsuśīlāḥ sādhavo yatra nārāyaṇaparāyaṇāḥ
translation: Gertainly this path (of devotion) is the correct and the best one in this world, as it is blissful (annihilating all miseries), and quite safe (with no danger or fear from anything). Kindly-disposed and desireless devotees of Lord Nārāyaṇa are found on this path (to help one in need, but there is nobody to do so in the paths of knowledge and action).

devanagari: प्रायश्चित्तानि चीर्णानि नारायणपराङ्मुखम् । न निष्पुनन्ति राजेन्द्र सुराकुम्भमिवापगाः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: prāyaścittāni cīrṇāni nārāyaṇaparāṅmukhamna niṣpunanti rājendra surākumbhamivāpagāḥ
translation: Oh King of Kings! Performance of all acts of expiation do not thoroughly purify a person who has set his face against Lord Nārāyaṇa, even as all the rivers (taken together) cannot purify a pitcher of wine.

devanagari: सकृन्मनः कृष्णपदारविन्दयोः । र्निवेशितं तद्‍गुणरागि यैरिह । न ते यमं पाशभृतश्च तद्‍भटान् । स्वन्स्वप्नेऽपि पश्यन्ति हि चीर्णनिष्कृताः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: sakṛnmanaḥ kṛṣṇapadāravindayoḥrniveśitaṃ tad‍guṇarāgi yairihana te yamaṃ pāśabhṛtaśca tad‍bhaṭānsvansvapne'pi paśyanti hi cīrṇaniṣkṛtāḥ
translation: Those who have even once in their life here, set on the lotus-like feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, their mind which felt an attachment for (and not knowledge or complete comprehension of) his excellent qualities, have by this (very temporary attachment and devotion) performed all‘expiatory acts. And they never see Yama (god of death) or his servants with a noose in their hands, even in their dreams.

devanagari: अत्र च उदाहरन्ति इमं इतिहासं पुरातनम् । दूतानां विष्णुयमयोः संवादस्तं निबोध मे ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: atra ca udāharanti imaṃ itihāsaṃ purātanamdūtānāṃ viṣṇuyamayoḥ saṃvādastaṃ nibodha me
translation: And on this topic, the learned one narrate (by way of illustration) this ancient legend consisting of a conversation between the messengers of Viṣṇu and those of Yama. Listen to it from me.

devanagari: कान्यकुब्जे द्विजः कश्चित् दासीपतिरजामिलः । नाम्ना नष्टसदाचारो दास्याः संसर्गदूषितः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: kānyakubje dvijaḥ kaścit dāsīpatirajāmilaḥnāmnā naṣṭasadācāro dāsyāḥ saṃsargadūṣitaḥ
translation: In (the city of) Kānyakubja (modern Kanauj), there lived a certain Brāhmaṇa called Ajāmila. He married a maid-servant and got polluted through his association with a female slave, and' he neglected the pious way of life (including the religious duties prescribed for a Brāhmaṇa).

devanagari: बन्द्यक्षैः कैतवैश्चौर्यैः गर्हितां वृत्तिमास्थितः । बिभ्रत्कुटुम्बं अशुचिः यातयामास देहिनः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: bandyakṣaiḥ kaitavaiścauryaiḥ garhitāṃ vṛttimāsthitaḥbibhratkuṭumbaṃ aśuciḥ yātayāmāsa dehinaḥ
translation: He maintained his family by adopting a reproachful way of life consisting of holding captives for ransom, gambling, committing frauds and thefts. That sinful fellow tortured creatures (before killing).

devanagari: एवं निवसतस्तस्य लालयानस्य तत्सुतान् । कालोऽत्यगान् महान् राजन् नष्टाशीत्यायुषः समाः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ nivasatastasya lālayānasya tatsutānkālo'tyagān mahān rājan naṣṭāśītyāyuṣaḥ samāḥ
translation: A long period consisting of eighty-eight years of his life thus elapsed in maintaining himself that way, and fondling the children of that Dāsa woman.

devanagari: तस्य प्रवयसः पुत्रा दश तेषां तु योऽवमः । बालो नारायणो नाम्ना पित्रोश्च दयितो भृशम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya pravayasaḥ putrā daśa teṣāṃ tu yo'vamaḥbālo nārāyaṇo nāmnā pitrośca dayito bhṛśam
translation: The old fellow (Ajāmila) had ten sons (by that woman). The youngest of them was a boy named Nārāyaṇa. He was extremely doted over by his parents.

devanagari: स बद्धहृदयस्तस्मिन् अर्भके कलभाषिणि । निरीक्षमाणस्तल्लीलां मुमुदे जरठो भृशम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa baddhahṛdayastasmin arbhake kalabhāṣiṇinirīkṣamāṇastallīlāṃ mumude jaraṭho bhṛśam
translation: He (Ajāmila) had set his heart on that sweet lisping child. The old man was highly delighted while witnessing the child’s plays.

devanagari: भुञ्जानः प्रपिबन् खादन् बालकं स्नेहयन्त्रितः । भोजयन् पाययन्मूढो न वेदागतमन्तकम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhuñjānaḥ prapiban khādan bālakaṃ snehayantritaḥbhojayan pāyayanmūḍho na vedāgatamantakam
translation: Bound by filial love for child, he used to feed it while he was eating, make it drink at the time of his drinking. In this way, the fool did not know that the hour of his death had arrived.

devanagari: स एवं वर्तमानोऽज्ञो मृत्युकाल उपस्थिते । मतिं चकार तनये बाले नारायणाह्वये ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa evaṃ vartamāno'jño mṛtyukāla upasthitematiṃ cakāra tanaye bāle nārāyaṇāhvaye
translation: Thus, the ignorant Ajāmila led his life. When the time of death actually arrived, he thought of his young son called Nārāyaṇa.

devanagari: स पाशहस्तान् त्रीन् दृष्ट्वा पुरुषान् अति दारुणान् । वक्रतुण्डान् ऊर्ध्वरोम्ण आत्मानं नेतुमागतान् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa pāśahastān trīn dṛṣṭvā puruṣān ati dāruṇānvakratuṇḍān ūrdhvaromṇa ātmānaṃ netumāgatān
translation: (Then) he saw that three extremely terrible male figures with wry faces, and with the hair on their bodies standing erect, and with nooses in their hands, Had come to take him away.

devanagari: दूरे क्रीडनकासक्तं पुत्रं नारायणाह्वयम् । प्लावितेन स्वरेणोच्चैः आजुहावाकुलेन्द्रियः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: dūre krīḍanakāsaktaṃ putraṃ nārāyaṇāhvayamplāvitena svareṇoccaiḥ ājuhāvākulendriyaḥ
translation: With his senses greatly agitated, he called out in a prolonged loud voice, his son named Nārāyaṇa who was absorbed in his play-things at a distance.

devanagari: निशम्य म्रियमाणस्य मुखतो हरिकीर्तनम् । भर्तुर्नाम महाराज पार्षदाः सहसाऽपतन् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: niśamya mriyamāṇasya mukhato harikīrtanambharturnāma mahārāja pārṣadāḥ sahasā'patan
translation: Having heard the repetition of the name of Lord Hari, their master, uttered by the dying man, the servants of Nārāyaṇa immediately rushed forward, Oh great King.

devanagari: विकर्षतोऽन्तर्हृदयाद् दासीपतिमजामिलम् । यमप्रेष्यान् विष्णुदूता वारयामासुरोजसा ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: vikarṣato'ntarhṛdayād dāsīpatimajāmilamyamapreṣyān viṣṇudūtā vārayāmāsurojasā
translation: Angels of Viṣṇu (intercepted and) forcibly stopped the messengers of Yama who were dragging (the jīva of) Ajāmila, the husband of the maid-servant, from the interior of his heart.

devanagari: ऊचुर्निषेधितास्तांस्ते वैवस्वतपुरःसराः । के यूयं प्रतिषेद्धारो धर्मराजस्य शासनम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: ūcurniṣedhitāstāṃste vaivasvatapuraḥsarāḥke yūyaṃ pratiṣeddhāro dharmarājasya śāsanam
translation: Thus prevented (from carrying out their duty) the messengers of Yama demanded, “Who are you who thus defy the order of Yama, the god presiding over righteousness.

devanagari: कस्य वा कुत आयाताः कस्मादस्य निषेधथ । किं देवा उपदेवा या यूयं किं सिद्धसत्तमाः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: kasya vā kuta āyātāḥ kasmādasya niṣedhathakiṃ devā upadevā yā yūyaṃ kiṃ siddhasattamāḥ
translation: Whose servants are you? Whence have you come? Why do you prevent his being taken away? Are you gods or demigods or are you some prominent Siddhas?

devanagari: सर्वे पद्मपलाशाक्षाः पीतकौशेयवाससः । किरीटिनः कुण्डलिनो लसत्पुष्करमालिनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: sarve padmapalāśākṣāḥ pītakauśeyavāsasaḥkirīṭinaḥ kuṇḍalino lasatpuṣkaramālinaḥ
translation: (Please do not get offended for our ignorance about you). All of you have eyes like lotus-petals, wear yellow silken garments and are adorned with diadems, ear-rings and shining lotus garlands.

devanagari: सर्वे च नूत्‍नवयसः सर्वे चारुचतुर्भुजाः । धनुर्निषङ्गासिगदा शङ्खचक्राम्बुजश्रियः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: sarve ca nūt‍navayasaḥ sarve cārucaturbhujāḥdhanurniṣaṅgāsigadā śaṅkhacakrāmbujaśriyaḥ
translation: You are all in the full bloom of youth. All of you possess four beautiful arms. (Each of) you appears majestic with (your lustrous) bow, quiver, sword, mace (gadā), conch, discus and lotus.

devanagari: दिशो वितिमिरालोकाः कुर्वन्तः स्वेन तेजसा । किमर्थं धर्मपालस्य किङ्करान्नो निषेधथ ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: diśo vitimirālokāḥ kurvantaḥ svena tejasākimarthaṃ dharmapālasya kiṅkarānno niṣedhatha
translation: By your personal splendour, you dispel darkness from all quarters and out-shine the lustre of others. Why do you prevent us, the obedient servants of Yama, the protector of the moral law?”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्युक्ते यमदूतैस्ते वासुदेवोक्तकारिणः । तान् प्रत्यूचुः प्रहस्येदं मेघनिर्ह्रादया गिरा ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaityukte yamadūtaiste vāsudevoktakāriṇaḥtān pratyūcuḥ prahasyedaṃ meghanirhrādayā girā
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When the obedient servants (who execute the commands) of Lord Vāsudeva were asked thus by the messengers of Yama, they laughed heartily, and replied as follows to them, in a voice deep like the thunder.

devanagari: श्रीविष्णुदूता ऊचुः । यूयं वै धर्मराजस्य यदि निर्देशकारिणः । ब्रूत धर्मस्य नस्तत्त्वं यच्च धर्मस्य लक्षणम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīviṣṇudūtā ūcuḥyūyaṃ vai dharmarājasya yadi nirdeśakāriṇaḥbrūta dharmasya nastattvaṃ yacca dharmasya lakṣaṇam
translation:
The Messengers of Viṣṇu replied:
If you are really the executors of the orders of the King of Righteousness, tell us what is the true nature of righteousness and what is the distinguishing character of Dharma.

devanagari: कथं स्विद् ध्रियते दण्डः किं वास्य स्थानमीप्सितम् । दण्ड्याः किं कारिणः सर्वे आहो स्वित् कतिचिन्नृणाम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: kathaṃ svid dhriyate daṇḍaḥ kiṃ vāsya sthānamīpsitamdaṇḍyāḥ kiṃ kāriṇaḥ sarve āho svit katicinnṛṇām
translation: How (on what principles) is punishment given? What (who) is regarded as the proper object? Who deserve punishments? Whether all of them are punishable or some of them?

devanagari: यमदूता ऊचुः । वेदप्रणिहितो धर्मो ह्यधर्मस्तद्विपर्ययः । वेदो नारायणः साक्षाय् स्वयम्भूः इति शुश्रुम ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: yamadūtā ūcuḥvedapraṇihito dharmo hyadharmastadviparyayaḥvedo nārāyaṇaḥ sākṣāy svayambhūḥ iti śuśruma
translation:
Servants of Yama replied:
What is ordained in the Vedas is dharma or righteousness. What is against the prescription of the Vedas is a- dharma (un-righteousness). We have heard it traditionally said that the Vedas are Nārāyaṇa incarnate and they, being the breath (flowing out naturally from the nostrils) of the Lord[6] are self-existent.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Cf. asya mahato bhūtasya niḥśvasitam etad yad Ṛgvedaḥ /

devanagari: येन स्वधाम्न्यमी भावा रजःसत्त्वतमोमयाः । गुणनामक्रियारूपैः विभाव्यन्ते यथातथम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: yena svadhāmnyamī bhāvā rajaḥsattvatamomayāḥguṇanāmakriyārūpaiḥ vibhāvyante yathātatham
translation: It is the Lord (Nārāyaṇa) by whom all these living beings which are constituted of the attributes sattva, rajas and tamas and which are severally discriminated and distinguished by their qualities (e.g. quiescence), designations (Brāhmaṇa, Kṣatriya etc.), activities (study, teaching etc.) and forms (social classes, stages of life—varṇas and āśramas), are supported properly in his own Form (Being).

devanagari: सूर्योऽग्निः खं मरुद्गावः सोमः सन्ध्याहनी दिशः । कं कुः स्वयं धर्म इति ह्येते दैह्यस्य साक्षिणः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: sūryo'gniḥ khaṃ marudgāvaḥ somaḥ sandhyāhanī diśaḥkaṃ kuḥ svayaṃ dharma iti hyete daihyasya sākṣiṇaḥ
translation: The Sun-god, the Fire-god, the Sky, the Wind-god, cows, the Moon-god, the twilights, days and nights, cardinal points, the presiding deities of Water and the Earth, Time and (the god) Dharma—all these are the witnesses (of the acts) of the embodied beings.

devanagari: एतैरधर्मो विज्ञातः स्थानं दण्डस्य युज्यते । सर्वे कर्मानुरोधेन दण्डमर्हन्ति कारिणः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: etairadharmo vijñātaḥ sthānaṃ daṇḍasya yujyatesarve karmānurodhena daṇḍamarhanti kāriṇaḥ
translation: (Acts of) unrighteousness as evidenced by these is judged to be the proper reason for awarding punishment. All the perpetrators of (evil) actions deserve punishment according to the nature of their acts.

devanagari: सम्भवन्ति हि भद्राणि विपरीतानि चानघाः । कारिणां गुणसङ्गोऽस्ति देहवान् न ह्यकर्मकृत् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: sambhavanti hi bhadrāṇi viparītāni cānaghāḥkāriṇāṃ guṇasaṅgo'sti dehavān na hyakarmakṛt
translation: Oh sinless ones! Actions, meritorious or sinful are being done by active persons as they are associated with attributes (like rajas, tamas). For nobody invested with a body can be actionless.

devanagari: येन यावान् यथाधर्मो धर्मो वेह समीहितः । स एव तत्फलं भुङ्क्ते तथा तावदमुत्र वै ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: yena yāvān yathādharmo dharmo veha samīhitaḥsa eva tatphalaṃ bhuṅkte tathā tāvadamutra vai
translation: The same person enjoys the fruit of the same sinful or a meritorious act in the next world in the same manner and to the same extent according to the manner and extent to which that (sinful or meritorious) act has been done by him in. this world.

devanagari: यथेह देवप्रवराः त्रैविध्यं उपलभ्यते । भूतेषु गुणवैचित्र्यात् तथान्यत्रानुमीयते ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: yatheha devapravarāḥ traividhyaṃ upalabhyatebhūteṣu guṇavaicitryāt tathānyatrānumīyate
translation: Oh great gods! (As you know it by your omniscience) Just as here (in this world) there is found a three-fold variety[7] in living beings due to the diversity in the peculiar qualities (or mixtures of sattva, rajas, and tamas attributes exhibited in their pious, sinful or mixed acts)^ the same may be inferred about them in the next birth[8]. (Otherwise such, a diversity cannot be explained).
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā states that this is the arthāpatti (circumstantial inference) to explain re-birth. He explains the three-fold variety as follows:
According to Padaratnāvalī, they are the best, worst and middle types of beings according to dominance of guṇas—sattva, tamas and rajas.

Footnote [8]:
In the next world.—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: वर्तमानोऽन्ययोः कालो गुणाभिज्ञापको यथा । एवं जन्मान्ययोरेतद् धर्माधर्मनिदर्शनम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: vartamāno'nyayoḥ kālo guṇābhijñāpako yathāevaṃ janmānyayoretad dharmādharmanidarśanam
translation: Just as the present period of time is the index of the characteristics of the other periods that precede and follow it[9], similarly the present life (of human beings, prognostically) indicates the righteous and sinful actions committed in the past and (likely to be committed) in the future existences as well.
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Just as the present season, e.g. the spring indicates the characteristics regarding flowers, fruits etc. of the past and future Spring seasons.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: मनसैव पुरे देवः पूर्वरूपं विपश्यति । अनुमीमांसतेऽपूर्वं मनसा भगवानजः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: manasaiva pure devaḥ pūrvarūpaṃ vipaśyatianumīmāṃsate'pūrvaṃ manasā bhagavānajaḥ
translation: [10] Staying in his own capital (Saṃyaminī), the god Yamadharma clearly visualizes by his mental power, the previous form (e.g. god, sub-human being or the merits, sins etc.) of the jīva (departed Soul), and deeply reflects what the jīva’s future state should be, by his reasoning capacity; for his Lordship is omniscient like god Brahmā.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
(i) Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: The Inner Controlling Self (antaryāmin) staying in the city in the form of body etc.
(ii) Bhāgavata Candrikā thinks that this verse refers to the Supreme Soul who stays as antaryāmin.
(iii) Padaratnāvalī: Lord Nārāyaṇa staying in the body of the living beings, sees the past and future form, as also the merits and sins of the Jīva.
(iv) Siddhāntapradīpa: It is the Supreme God (Parameśvara) abiding in the body yet unaffected by birth etc. connected with the physical body, dispenses the fruits of merits and sins through god Yama and he never interferes.

devanagari: यथाज्ञस्तमसा युक्त उपास्ते व्यक्तमेव हि । न वेद पूर्वमपरं नष्टजन्मस्मृतिस्तथा ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: yathājñastamasā yukta upāste vyaktameva hina veda pūrvamaparaṃ naṣṭajanmasmṛtistathā
translation: Just as a sleeping person in the dream-state regards that he is identical with the body manifested in the dream, and does not know his body in the pre-dream state or the body to be assumed in the next dream, similarly the ignorant jīva conditioned by avidyā knows his present body as manifested by the actions of his previous existence and not the previous form assumed in the past incarnation nor the future body (of his futṇre existence) due to the loss of memory of his past lives.

devanagari: पञ्चभिः कुरुते स्वार्थान् पञ्च वेदाथ पञ्चभिः । एकस्तु षोडशेन त्रीन् स्वयं सप्तदशोऽश्नुते ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: pañcabhiḥ kurute svārthān pañca vedātha pañcabhiḥekastu ṣoḍaśena trīn svayaṃ saptadaśo'śnute
translation: With five conative organs, the jīva carries out his (physical) functions (e.g. locomotion, exchange); with five cognitive senses, he perceives (knows) the five objects of senses. The single jīva, the seventeenth, combined with the sixteenth, i.e. the mind[11] experiences all alone, three-fold objects presented by the cognitive senses, conative organs and the mind.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
And the other fifteen constituents are the ten sense-organs, five subtle bhūtas forming the bases of objects of senses—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: तदेतत् षोडशकलं लिङ्गं शक्तित्रयं महत् । धत्तेऽनुसंसृतिं पुंसि हर्षशोकभयार्तिदाम् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: tadetat ṣoḍaśakalaṃ liṅgaṃ śaktitrayaṃ mahatdhatte'nusaṃsṛtiṃ puṃsi harṣaśokabhayārtidām
translation: This subtle-body (liṅga [liṅgam]) constituted of the sixteen parts (enumerated above) and possessing three potencies (of the attributes sattva, rajas and tamas) is beginningless. It continues to hold down a man in saṃsāra which gives him joy, grief, fear and sufferings.

devanagari: देह्यज्ञोऽजितषड्वर्गो नेच्छन् कर्माणि कार्यते । कोशकार इवात्मानं कर्मणाऽच्छाद्य मुह्यति ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: dehyajño'jitaṣaḍvargo necchan karmāṇi kāryatekośakāra ivātmānaṃ karmaṇā'cchādya muhyati
translation: The ignorant embodied being who has not subdued the group of six (viz. five cognitive senses and the mind is forced (by his subtle body) to perform actions despite his un- willingness. Like a silk-worm covering itself in its cocoon, he covers (entangles) himself in the net of karmas and fails to see the way out.

devanagari: न हि कश्चित् क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत् । कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म गुणैः स्वाभाविकैर्बलात् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: na hi kaścit kṣaṇamapi jātu tiṣṭhatyakarmakṛtkāryate hyavaśaḥ karma guṇaiḥ svābhāvikairbalāt
translation: Verily, nobody can ever remain actionless even for a moment; for everyone is helpless and is forced to do some act by passions like attachments etc. born of (the three) attributes (viz. sattva, rajas and tamas) which are the results of the impressions of past actions.[12]
purport:
Footnote [12]:
This is verbatim the same as Bhagavad Gītā 3.5

devanagari: लब्ध्वा निमित्तमव्यक्तं व्यक्ताव्यक्तं भवत्युत । यथायोनि यथाबीजं स्वभावेन बलीयसा ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: labdhvā nimittamavyaktaṃ vyaktāvyaktaṃ bhavatyutayathāyoni yathābījaṃ svabhāvena balīyasā
translation: With avyakta (unmanifested destiny—adṛṣṭa) as the instrumental cause, the gross physical body and the subtle liṅga-śarīra are formed. According to the powerful impulses of the jīva, he becomes moulded like his mother’s form or like his father’s personality.

devanagari: एष प्रकृतिसङ्गेन पुरुषस्य विपर्ययः । आसीत्स एव न चिराद् ईशसङ्गाद् विलीयते ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa prakṛtisaṅgena puruṣasya viparyayaḥāsītsa eva na cirād īśasaṅgād vilīyate
translation: This contrary state of the Puruṣa (viz. involvement of the (puruṣa into saṃsāra, miseries suffered despite the Soul’s blissful nature, due to his false identity with his body) was due to the contact with Prakṛti. It (this contact) dissolves completely within a short time through his devotion to God.

devanagari: अयं हि श्रुतसम्पन्नः शीलवृत्तगुणालयः । धृतव्रतो मृदुर्दान्तः सत्यवान् मंत्रविच्छुचिः ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ hi śrutasampannaḥ śīlavṛttaguṇālayaḥdhṛtavrato mṛdurdāntaḥ satyavān maṃtravicchuciḥ
translation: This (Ajāmila) was really studied in the Vedic lore; (was) the very abode of good nature, righteous conduct and virtue; was a strict observer of vows, gentle, self-controlled truthful, master of mystical formulae, pure (both in mind and body).

devanagari: गुर्वग्न्यतिथिवृद्धानां शुश्रूषुर्निरहङ्कृतः । सर्वभूतसुहृत्साधुः मिर्मितवागनसूयकः ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: gurvagnyatithivṛddhānāṃ śuśrūṣurnirahaṅkṛtaḥsarvabhūtasuhṛtsādhuḥ mirmitavāganasūyakaḥ
translation: He used to serve his preceptors, file sacred fire, the guests and elderly relatives advanced in age. He was untouched by pride (full of humility), a friend to all living beings, a man of few words; he was a saintly person, not jealous of anyone.

devanagari: एकदासौ वनं यातः पितृसन्देशकृद् द्विजः । आदाय तत आवृत्तः फलपुष्पसमित्कुशान् ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadāsau vanaṃ yātaḥ pitṛsandeśakṛd dvijaḥādāya tata āvṛttaḥ phalapuṣpasamitkuśān
translation: One day, this Brāhmaṇa who obediently used to carry out his father’s orders, went to the forest. He returned therefrom taking with him fruits, flowers, sticks for sacrificial fire and Kuśa grass.

devanagari: ददर्श कामिनं कञ्चित् शूद्रं सह भुजिष्यया । पीत्वा च मधु मैरेयं मदाघूर्णितनेत्रया ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: dadarśa kāminaṃ kañcit śūdraṃ saha bhujiṣyayāpītvā ca madhu maireyaṃ madāghūrṇitanetrayā
translation: On his way back, he saw a certain shameless, libidinous Śūdra who had drunk wine (called Maireya—distilled from meal) in the company of a harlot whose eyes were also swimming due to intoxication. With that woman who was standing by him, with the knot of her Sari loosened (hence in a semi-naked condition), the shameless Śūdra was courting, and singing and joking nearby.

devanagari: मत्तया विश्लथन्नीव्या व्यपेतं निरपत्रपम् । क्रीडन्तं अनुगायन्तं हसन्तमनयान्तिके ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: mattayā viślathannīvyā vyapetaṃ nirapatrapamkrīḍantaṃ anugāyantaṃ hasantamanayāntike
translation: On his way back, he saw a certain shameless, libidinous Śūdra who had drunk wine (called Maireya—distilled from meal) in the company of a harlot whose eyes were also swimming due to intoxication. With that woman who was standing by him, with the knot of her Sari loosened (hence in a semi-naked condition), the shameless Śūdra was courting, and singing and joking nearby.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा तां कामलिप्तेन बाहुना परिरम्भिताम् । जगाम हृच्छयवशं सहसैव विमोहितः ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā tāṃ kāmaliptena bāhunā parirambhitāmjagāma hṛcchayavaśaṃ sahasaiva vimohitaḥ
translation: Seeing her embraced in arms which were erotically painted with unguents, Ajāmila was instantaneously infatuated and succumbed to passionate love.

devanagari: स्तम्भयन् आत्मनात्मानं यावत्सत्त्वं यथाश्रुतम् । न शशाक समाधातुं मनो मदनवेपितम् ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: stambhayan ātmanātmānaṃ yāvatsattvaṃ yathāśrutamna śaśāka samādhātuṃ mano madanavepitam
translation: Despite his attempts to control himself with all (the power of) his reasoning capacity and scholarship, he was unable to stabilize his mind and calm down his heart, which was agitated by the god of Love.

devanagari: तन्निमित्तस्मरव्याज ग्रहग्रस्तो विचेतनः । तामेव मनसा ध्यायन् स्वधर्माद् विरराम ह ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: tannimittasmaravyāja grahagrasto vicetanaḥtāmeva manasā dhyāyan svadharmād virarāma ha
translation: Being possessed by the devil in the form of passionate desire for her, he lost (the balance of) his mind. Brooding over her in his mind, he desisted from performing his prescribed religious duties.

devanagari: तामेव तोषयामास पित्र्येणार्थेन यावता । ग्राम्यैर्मनोरमैः कामैः प्रसीदेत यथा तथा ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: tāmeva toṣayāmāsa pitryeṇārthena yāvatāgrāmyairmanoramaiḥ kāmaiḥ prasīdeta yathā tathā
translation: With his entire ancestral property, he tried to propitiate her by vulgar carnal pleasures (appealing to her) so that she may be pleased with him.

devanagari: विप्रां स्वभार्यामप्रौढां कुले महति लम्भिताम् । विससर्जाचिरात्पापः स्वैरिण्यापाङ्गविद्धधीः ॥ ६५ ॥
english_devnagari: viprāṃ svabhāryāmaprauḍhāṃ kule mahati lambhitāmvisasarjācirātpāpaḥ svairiṇyāpāṅgaviddhadhīḥ
translation: The sinful fellow whose judgment was paralysed by the side-glances of that wanton woman, soon abandoned his regularly wedded young Brāhmaṇa wife who was born in a good family.

devanagari: यतस्ततश्चोपनिन्ये न्यायतोऽन्यायतो धनम् । बभारास्याः कुटुम्बिन्याः कुटुम्बं मन्दधीरयम् ॥ ६६ ॥
english_devnagari: yatastataścopaninye nyāyato'nyāyato dhanambabhārāsyāḥ kuṭumbinyāḥ kuṭumbaṃ mandadhīrayam
translation: This stupid fellow (after squandering his ancestral property) got money from any place, by fair means or foul, and maintained the family of this woman (now his housewife).

devanagari: यदसौ शास्त्रमुल्लङ्घ्य स्वैरचार्यतिगर्हितः । अवर्तत चिरं कालं अघायुः अशुचिर्मलात् ॥ ६७ ॥
english_devnagari: yadasau śāstramullaṅghya svairacāryatigarhitaḥavartata ciraṃ kālaṃ aghāyuḥ aśucirmalāt
translation: Inasmuch as this fellow violated all the Śāstric injunctions and led a licentious life censured by noble persons, and lived a sinful life for a long time in an impure condition and eating dirty (polluted by a harlot’s touch) food, we (servants of Yama-dharma) shall therefore carry this sinner who did nothing for expiating his sins, to the presence of Yama (the god wielding the rod of punishment). There he will get purified through (undergoing the prescribed) punishment.

devanagari: तत एनं दण्डपाणेः सकाशं कृतकिल्बिषम् । नेष्यामोऽकृतनिर्वेशं यत्र दण्डेन शुद्ध्यति ॥ ६८ ॥
english_devnagari: tata enaṃ daṇḍapāṇeḥ sakāśaṃ kṛtakilbiṣamneṣyāmo'kṛtanirveśaṃ yatra daṇḍena śuddhyati
translation:
Inasmuch as this fellow violated all the Śāstric injunctions and led a licentious life censured by noble persons, and lived a sinful life for a long time in an impure condition and eating dirty (polluted by a harlot’s touch) food, we (servants of Yama-dharma) shall therefore carry this sinner who did nothing for expiating his sins, to the presence of Yama (the god wielding the rod of punishment). There he will get purified through (undergoing the prescribed) punishment.
